description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with irregular, naturalistic surfaces and a sandy beige coloration. The block has uneven edges and corners, with some areas appearing chipped or eroded, giving it a weathered look. One side features a patch of white residue or mineral deposit, suggesting exposure to moisture or environmental elements. The top surface is relatively flat but not perfectly smooth, showing subtle undulations and textural variation. A small yellow marking or sticker is visible on one upper edge, possibly for identification or inventory purposes. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian function, likely as a barrier or boundary element in an outdoor urban or landscaped setting.
description: A weathered, sandy beige stone block with irregular surfaces, chipped edges, and a patch of white residue. It has a flat top and a small yellow marking on one edge.
description_view_0: This view shows the broad, flat face of the block with rough, uneven texture and a prominent corner; the yellow marking is faintly visible on the top edge.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the white residue or mineral deposit running down one end, with the yellow marking more clearly visible on the upper surface.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the block's irregular, chipped end and the continuation of the white residue, with the yellow marking on the top surface.
description_view_3: The opposite side reveals a relatively unblemished, rough surface with the yellow marking still visible, and no significant residue present.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible chipping and erosion on edges, with a patch of white residue indicating mineral buildup or water exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A small yellow marking or sticker is visible; no engravings or carvings present.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Isolated, likely used as a standalone barrier or boundary element.
